body.title: [sprint] Outcome summary at top of closed tickets (populate result, render first)
body.description:
**Problem:** A ticket's outcome — what actually got built, what diverged, the gotcha — is invisible at read time. The `result` field exists but sits at the bottom and is usually null, so a fresh reader sees intent (description) not reality. This is the stale-first-read class of bug (cf. the Hubert/Auditor mislabel that propagated from a ticket head).

**Proposed shape:** On ticket close, populate `result` with a 1-3 sentence outcome summary and render it at the TOP of the rendered ticket, above the description, clearly labeled (e.g. `## Outcome (as-closed)`). The head digest is written at the done-moment; a closed ticket is terminal, so the digest stays true.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (close path + ticket renderer).
**Design rules:** Log the close state change + result write (CLAUDE.md structural rules). Leading digest is safe only on terminal artifacts — a closed ticket qualifies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — populate result on close, render at top. OUT — backfilling result on already-closed tickets; schema changes beyond using the existing result field.
**Completion criteria:** Closing a ticket with a result string renders that result as a labeled head section above the description in `cc_queue.py show`; a ticket closed without a result is flagged. Verified by closing a test ticket and inspecting `show` output.
**Test plan:** Close a ticket with a result string; re-render via `show`; the outcome appears at the top and matches the body.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
[error] PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write): The skeleton must never depend on Postgres — skeleto — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-004 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-004: Flat-File Registry (Atomic Write))
…and 37 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that a fresh reader of any closed ticket sees its true outcome first, without reading the body.
